scope: Youth Scout release-candidate interactivity and rendered gameplay
release_standard: Every product and rendered-design category must score at least 9.0 from verified behavior
verdict_reason: Core interaction and system cohesion are strong, but overall rendered design and gameplay interactivity are 8.9, and manual accessibility, moderated usability, low-end performance, comparative history, actor breadth, and packaged-runtime gates remain open.

rendered_evidence.academy_case_capture_set: Supplemental desktop/mobile captures include recruitment-identity briefing states

implemented_systems: Responsive gameplay shell and safe-area mobile navigation
Interactive Observation Pitch with pointer, touch, keyboard, and synchronized list
Visual Week Journey with Commitment, Context, and Consequence beats
Evidence Board with hypotheses, claims, conflicts, unknowns, and source calibration
Visible club recruitment identities and authored-evidence opportunity fit
International assignment deliverables and exactly-once grading
Manager and board political meetings with memory, cooldowns, costs, and directives
Actor-specific special-event memories and delayed obligations
Consequence Cinema and Career Story Reel
Interactive bounded World Archive with season and public-player drill-through
Compact non-blocking achievement notifications and constrained tooltips
Seeded replayability telemetry using semantic divergence

verification.academy_case_visual_stories: 1/1 passed with desktop/mobile recruitment-identity evidence, Axe, document overflow, and main-region overflow checks
verification.world_archive_stories: 1/1 passed with desktop/mobile renders, Axe, dialog semantics, focus containment, Escape focus restoration, mobile season selection, player drill-through, and overflow checks
verification.youth_browser_suite: 45/45 passed on the first attempt in approximately 5.3 minutes

metric_definitions.broad_event_type_adjacent_repeat: adjacentEventTypeRepeatRate; broad taxonomy reuse, including distinct stages of one chain

long_career_evidence.deterministic_persistence_replay: passed for release-soak-01 with matching final digest
long_career_evidence.scope_note: Proves boundary rollover, bounded archive retention, save-growth limits, isolated-process memory ceilings, and deterministic persistence; does not replace the separate ten-season every-week browser soak or low-end runtime profiling.

verified_defects_closed: Mobile Live Observation clipping and inaccessible controls
Observation focus control with no authoritative effect
Quick-choice branch persistence and duplicate reward risk
Cosmetic and repeat-farmable Youth political interactions
Political radio semantics, focus, target size, and contrast
Transfer evaluation based on fabricated participation and hidden truth
International rewards disconnected from destination deliverables
Unreachable career path and missing organic leadership progression
Club recruitment modifiers operating without an explanation at the decision point
World history existing only as transient or static information
Achievement toasts obscuring gameplay and tooltips creating overflow
remaining_release_blockers: Report and Planner progressive disclosure to reduce vertical sprawl
Side-by-side club, manager, and player timeline comparison
Recurring interactive memory and conflicts for agents, families, journalists, employees, and individual rival scouts
Greater venue, portrait, event-treatment, motion, and audio variety
Moderated 12-player usability study with median SUS at least 85
Low-end mobile and desktop frame-time plus store-subscription profiling
Packaged save, offline, recovery, installer, Steam fallback, cloud-conflict, interrupted-write, and supported-platform proof
